rag_vectors.name: Notable per-family oddities
rag_vectors.text:
# [research/decomp/action-stream-extraction-audit.md] Notable per-family oddities
## Notable per-family oddities

- **Dash rams play two channels**: phase 0 arms mask 1 (slot = `+0x6ee` cursor, sometimes
  biased, e.g. FUN_8006fdf4's `+0x6ee + 6`) and mask 2 (fixed overlay slot), not mask 0xf
  (FUN_8006fb8c chunk_0009.c:4249-4250). Emitted as primary + `additionalStreams`.
- **Combo ladders inside one action**: pl0004's charge phase 0 seeds `+0x6ea=0`, plays s0,
  then inline-calls the next phase which plays s1 (FUN_80162804/zz_01628d4_,
  chunk_0042.c:756-841) — handled by per-path call sequencing (`pseq`), follow-ups emitted as
  `comboFollowUps`.
- **Runtime variant redirects**: FUN_8018e0f8-style leaves set `+0x581=5` and jump to the v5
  leaf when `(+0x5e0 & 0x2000)` (jump state) — the redirect target is walked as its own
  variant (`discoveredVia: runtime +0x581 redirect`), the flag-set stream also lands in
  `conditional`.
- **Borg-id-conditioned seeds inside shared handlers**: FUN_8018e888 (G RED family air-B)
  seeds `+0x6ea` = 1 for 0x629 / 4 for 0x62a / 0 otherwise; pl0100's family dash adds 0xe for
  id 0x108 (zz_00724c8_ chunk_0010.c:301-305). Handled by concrete borg-id emulation.
- **Interleaved action-script banks**: 0x8032744c's group-offset array starts 12 bytes into
  0x80327440's and both share slot tables. A "next table" bound therefore truncates slot
  counts (0x80327440 g3 is 8 slots, not 3); the join walks each slot table to its -1 sentinel
  instead. NOTE: `scripts/gen-melee-anim-kinds.mjs` `parseBank` still uses the next-table
  bound and silently drops streams from such banks (pl0402/pl0408's g3 s3..s7 exist but are
  missing from meleeAnimKinds.json).
- **Charge configs carry setup callbacks** (config word 4/5, e.g. G RED's 0x8018eda8/
  0x8018edc4) which the chain scanner correctly rejects (they don't store `+0x581`); the real
  chain overrides (FUN_8018ded0 → v6, FUN_8018dfac → v7) re-issue a command via zz_006a3d0_
  and then store the target variant.
- **pl0a0x (giant/gattai) dash chains** pass slot -1 on channel 0 alongside mask-4 calls —
  left unresolved rather than guessed (slot -1 semantics unproven).

# [research/decomp/action-vm-and-gcrash-decode-2026-07-05.md] Action-script VM + G Crash state machine decode (2026-07-05)
# Action-script VM + G Crash state machine decode (2026-07-05)

Question: the port generic-archetypes X-specials (projectile/aoe/melee). To port the real
source mechanics we needed (a) the 10 undecoded action-script stream opcodes and (b) the
per-family state handler that drives G RED's X-special "G Crash" motion. Both decoded this
session from `research/decomp/ghidra-export/*.c` + `user-data/GG4E/disc/sys/boot.dol`.

rag_vectors.name: Section A — Stream VM opcodes 0x02-0x07, 0x0b, 0x0c fully decoded
rag_vectors.text:
# [research/decomp/action-vm-and-gcrash-decode-2026-07-05.md] Section A — Stream VM opcodes 0x02-0x07, 0x0b, 0x0c fully decoded
## Section A — Stream VM opcodes 0x02-0x07, 0x0b, 0x0c fully decoded

The interpreter is `zz_004cd24_` @ `chunk_0006.c:1996`, dispatching on the 18-entry handler
table `PTR_FUN_802d0da0`. The anim-sound decode doc had marked 0x02-0x07, 0x0b, 0x0c, 0x11 as
"not decompiled" but op 0x11 (`FUN_8004cccc`) IS in `chunk_0006.c:1973`. The remaining 8 were
disassembled from boot.dol with `scratchpad/ppc_disasm.py` (PPC32 decoder; corpus at
0x8004c424..0x8004c7fc).

**Calling convention for every handler:** `void handler(int actor /*r3*/, uint partMask /*r4*/, int *streamPtrPtr /*r5*/)`. The first action is always `lwz r6, 0(r5)` (load stream ptr), a
`rlwinm. r0, r4, 0, N, N` (test one bit of partMask — which bit selects the opcode's
part-gating), then `addi r0, r6, <size>; stw r0, 0(r5)` (advance stream by record size), and
`bclr` if the part bit is clear. Record layout is `[op, b1, b2, b3, ...]`.

| op | size | part bit | Operand effect (DERIVED — every store confirmed in the disasm) |
|----|------|----------|---------------------------------------------------------------|
| 0x02 | 8 | bit 0 (part 0 only) | b2 → actor+0x1cef (part-0 state+0x1b), b3 → +0x1cf0, b4 → +0x6e8, b5 → +0x6e9. b1 selects a follow-up on actor+0x582: b1==-2 toggles (+0x582 ^= 1), b1==-1 leaves it, else +0x582 = b1. **The +0x1cef write is the "contact/hit-connected" flag the G Crash phase-1 handler reads (see Section B).** |
| 0x03 | 8 | bit 1 | b1 → +0x1d0f (part-1 state+0x1b), b2 → +0x1d10. If halfword b6..b7 (u16) != 0xffff: reads word +0x5e0, clears bits 20..27 (`rlwinm 0,28,19`), then ORs in `(u16 b6..b7) & 0xff00` (mask `rlwinm 0,20,27`). So op 0x03 sets a byte-nibble in the +0x5e0 control word that downstream state handlers gate on (e.g. FUN_8018e888 line 798 tests `+0x5e0 & 0xa0`). |
| 0x04 | 4 | bit 2 | b1 → +0x1d2f (part-2 state+0x1b), b2 → +0x1d30. Per-part flag setter. |
| 0x05 | 4 | bit 3 | b1 → +0x1d4f (part-3 state+0x1b), b2 → +0x1d50. Per-part flag setter. |
| 0x06 | 2 (post-lhau) | any of bits 0..3 | Reads a u16 (`lhau r8, 2(r7)`) — the halfword is the per-part TARGET value. For each part bit set in partMask, writes 1 to +0x1cf2 (part 0) / +0x1d12 (part 1) / +0x1d32 (part 2) / +0x1d52 (part 3). These are the per-part "go" flags consumed by the part-anim blenders. |
| 0x07 | 4 | bit 0 | b2 → conditional sthx (halfword store); on no-target path reads `*(*(actor+0x4b0) + 0xbe)` and `+0xbf` (the family descriptor's two "default hand" bytes) and stores them to +0x1d74/+0x1d75; then copies +0x1d74→+0x1d8c and +0x1d75→+0x1d8d. **Hand/weapon-swap op**: switches which weapon model is attached per part for the duration of the move. |

rag_vectors.name: Section A — Stream VM opcodes 0x02-0x07, 0x0b, 0x0c fully decoded
rag_vectors.text:
# [research/decomp/action-vm-and-gcrash-decode-2026-07-05.md] Section A — Stream VM opcodes 0x02-0x07, 0x0b, 0x0c fully decoded
| 0x0b | 4 | bit 1 | b2 is a bitmask over actor+0x709. If b1 == 0: `+0x709 \|= b2` (set bits). Else: `+0x709 &= ~b2` (clear bits). +0x709 is a generic per-actor flag byte (movement / FX-gating). |
| 0x0c | 4 | bit 0 | b2 is a bitmask over actor+0x57a AND +0x57b. If b1 == 0: `+0x57b \|= b2`. Else: clear b2 from BOTH +0x57a and +0x57b. +0x57a/+0x57b are flag bytes consumed by the state dispatcher (`chunk_0009.c:4097/4100` writes them for state transitions). |

**Conclusion A:** NONE of these 8 opcodes directly writes velocity (no stores to +0x44/+0x48/+0x4c
or +0x38). They are **per-part STATE / FLAG / WEAPON-SWAP setters** that configure the actor's
sub-state machines, FX flags, and hit-contact latches. The actual MOTION (velocity, position
integration) is in the **per-family state handlers** (Section B), NOT in the action-stream
bytecode. The stream is the choreography (anim + hitbox timing + state flags); the family
handler is the physics driver.

This is why generic-archetype porting cannot reproduce real move motion: the motion is in C
code unique to each borg family, not in shared data tables.

rag_vectors.name: Section B — G RED "G Crash" X-special is a 4-phase family state machine
rag_vectors.text:
# [research/decomp/action-vm-and-gcrash-decode-2026-07-05.md] Section B — G RED "G Crash" X-special is a 4-phase family state machine
## Section B — G RED "G Crash" X-special is a 4-phase family state machine

Source: `chunk_0047.c:777-970`. Reached via the cue chain (`cue-script-stream-decode-2026-07-04.md`):
command type 2 (X) → `+0x4f0` cue table entry 44 → state `[61, 0]` → `FUN_80065dfc`
→ `(**(code**)(actor+0x4b4))()` → G RED root `zz_018d24c_` actionIndex-2 entry
**`FUN_8018e888`**. Air-B shares this handler (same actionIndex 2). Phase table is
`0x80365844`; `+0x540` is the phase index. Each phase fn increments `+0x540` to advance.

rag_vectors.name: Phase 0 — `FUN_8018e888` (launch / start the stream)
Phase 1 — `FUN_8018ea54` (airborne physics + hit check)
Phase 2 — `FUN_8018eb2c` (descend with angle-steered XZ)
Phase 3 — `FUN_8018ec34` (land + return to idle)

rag_vectors.name: Section C — The complete G Crash motion picture
rag_vectors.text:
# [research/decomp/action-vm-and-gcrash-decode-2026-07-05.md] Section C — The complete G Crash motion picture
## Section C — The complete G Crash motion picture

Reconciling with the user's description ("fly up in the air with fire on his fist, then launches
forward with his fist"):

1. **Frame 0 (phase 0):** G Red gets vertical impulse Y=+4.0 (the "fly up"), is repositioned
   5% further from his lock target (windup spacing), and starts the g4 s0 choreography stream.
   The kind-15 hitbox is armed at frame 0 of the stream; the played anim `g04_s00` is the
   81-frame "fist chambered overhead" pose (the action-stream decode proves g4 s0 — NOT
   `special_s1` — is what the command-table X-record runs; the renderer-side
   `borgPresentationAssets.ts` override to `special_s1` is a TUNED mismatch).
2. **Frames 1..N (phase 1):** gravity (1.0) integrates, G Red arcs upward then falls while the
   kind-15 hitbox is live (records 33 @ frames 8-16 close-body, 21 @ frames 53-64 extended-fist;
   the latter is the "fist reached full extension" window).
3. **On hit connect (`+0x1cef > 0`):** G Red dashes forward at magnitude 60.0 with Y=10.0 — the
   "launches forward with his fist". Phase 2 begins.
4. **Phase 2-3:** angle-steered descent, lands, returns to idle.

**Why the port's G Crash is broken:** `combat.ts` `startSpecialAttack` for `archetype === "projectile"`
calls `spawnSpecialProjectiles` which spawns a single forward-velocity "energy" bolt and never
touches the actor's velocity, never advances a 4-phase state machine, and never reads the kind-15
hit windows. The X-special cooldown gates re-presses but no motion happens. Visually G Red plays
his `special_s1` clip (26 frames, stationary) and an energy bolt flies out — the user's
"missing his crash attack" and "doesn't match source".

rag_vectors.name: Section D — Porting recipe (the real-mechanics port the user asked for)
rag_vectors.text:
# [research/decomp/action-vm-and-gcrash-decode-2026-07-05.md] Section D — Porting recipe (the real-mechanics port the user asked for)
## Section D — Porting recipe (the real-mechanics port the user asked for)

The motion is per-family code. To port G Crash 1:1 the work is:

1. **New `SpecialDriver` interface in @gf/combat** keyed by `(borgId, button)` returning either
   the existing generic archetype OR a per-family driver. The G RED family driver covers
   pl0615/pl0629/pl062a (and any other family that shares ctor `0x8018ccfc`).
2. **Port `FUN_8018e888..FUN_8018ec34` as a 4-phase state machine** on `BorgRuntime`:
   - Add `xSpecialPhase: 0|1|2|3`, `xSpecialTimer`, `xSpecialAngle` to `BorgRuntime.cooldowns`.
   - Phase 0 (`startSpecialAttack` for the G RED family): set `b.vel.y = 4.0` (impulse),
     reposition `b.pos` along `(b.pos − targetPos) × 0.05` (the 5%-further windup), arm the
     kind-15 hitbox via the existing melee-hit-resolution path (reuse `startMeleeAttack`'s
     active-window machinery with the kind-15 record windows), play anim `g4 s0` (the renderer
     override for pl0615 special should switch back to `g04_s00`/`down_s0` now that the source
     is decoded), set `xSpecialPhase = 1`.
   - Phase 1 (per-frame while `xSpecialPhase === 1`): apply gravity 1.0, integrate; if the
     kind-15 hit connected this frame (track via a per-swing `hitConnected` flag like melee),
     set `xSpecialPhase = 2`, `b.vel = {0, 10, 60}` in the lock-target's direction.
   - Phase 2: steer XZ by a ticking yaw angle; on hit-end (`+0x1cef < 0` equivalent) advance
     to phase 3 with timer 120.
   - Phase 3: tick angle by `(angle − 176) × 0.9`; on ground contact clear flags, return to idle.
3. **The kind-15 hit windows** (`attackHitTables.json` pl0615 kinds[15] = [33, 21]) drive the
   damage/hitstun via the existing melee-hit path — no new hitbox code needed, just route the
   X-special through `startMeleeAttack`'s resolver with the kind-15 leaf substituted for the
   ground-ladder step.
4. **Constants**: lift `G_CRASH = { LAUNCH_Y: 4.0, RELPOS_SCALE: 0.95, GRAVITY: 1.0,
   HIT_DASH_XZ: 60.0, HIT_DASH_Y: 10.0, LAND_TIMER: 120.0, ANGLE_DECAY: 0.9, ANGLE_BASE: 176.0 }`
   into `constants.ts` as DERIVED (every value read from boot.dol this session).
5. **Other families**: each borg family with a unique X-special handler (Star Hero's ramming
   dash `FUN_801121fc` family, Sword Knight's, etc.) needs its own driver ported the same way.
   The generic `archetype: "projectile"` stays as the fallback for families whose handlers
   haven't been ported yet.

rag_vectors.name: Section D — Porting recipe (the real-mechanics port the user asked for)
rag_vectors.text:
# [research/decomp/action-vm-and-gcrash-decode-2026-07-05.md] Section D — Porting recipe (the real-mechanics port the user asked for)
The action-stream bytecode itself does NOT need a runtime interpreter port — its motion ops are
state/flag setters (Section A), and the real motion lives in the per-family C state machines.
The streams' value is the choreography (anim + hitbox-arm timing + the contact-flag writes the
state machine polls); those are already decoded into `meleeAnimKinds.json` + `attackHitTables.json`.

rag_vectors.name: Anim-driven sound decode (2026-07-04)
rag_vectors.text:
# [research/decomp/anim-sound-op-decode-2026-07-04.md] Anim-driven sound decode (2026-07-04)
# Anim-driven sound decode (2026-07-04)

Question: which action-script stream opcode(s) trigger sounds, and where does the sound id
live? TWO results, both proven this session:

1. **Stream op 0x0e IS a playSound opcode with a literal id in its operand — but the GG4E
   boot.dol action-script banks NEVER use it** (0 occurrences across all 114 known banks /
   12031 stream slots; full op histogram below). Honest negative for stream-level extraction.
2. **The REAL carrier of per-move swing audio is the per-ANIM sound-event table at
   `actor+0x4e8`** (the "PATH B" combat-se-ids.json pointed at) — and it is STATIC boot.dol
   data joined to the anims the streams play, so it IS statically extractable after all.
   Format fully decoded + validated on G RED below; this is what the port now extracts and
   wires.

rag_vectors.name: Method
rag_vectors.text:
# [research/decomp/anim-sound-op-decode-2026-07-04.md] Method
## Method

The borg action-script interpreter dispatches stream ops through the handler pointer table at
`0x802d0da0` (18 entries, ops 0x00..0x11). Every handler body was checked for calls into the
sound layer (`zz_00efb3c_` @0x800efb3c dispatcher, or wrappers `zz_00f036c_`/`zz_00f061c_`/
`zz_00f0104_`/`zz_00f0f84_` that reach it):

1. Handlers present in the decomp corpus were read directly (chunk_0006.c:1609-1938).
2. Handlers MISSING from the corpus (ops 0x02-0x07, 0x0b, 0x0c, 0x11) were verified by raw
   PPC `bl`-target scan of boot.dol over each handler's address range (scratchpad script,
   this session). One further level was scanned for every non-sound helper the handlers call.

rag_vectors.name: Op 0x0e — playSound (defined by the engine, UNUSED by the data)
rag_vectors.text:
# [research/decomp/anim-sound-op-decode-2026-07-04.md] Op 0x0e — playSound (defined by the engine, UNUSED by the data)
## Op 0x0e — playSound (defined by the engine, UNUSED by the data)

Handler `FUN_8004c97c` (chunk_0006.c:1826-1859). 4-byte record, `*streamPtr += 4`.

Operand layout:

| byte | meaning |
|------|---------|
| 0 | opcode 0x0e |
| 1 | part index 0..3 — handler gates on `partMask & (1 << byte1)` (chunk_0006.c:1835). Part 0 is the main body script; multi-part borgs address a specific part. |
| 2 | mode (see below) |
| 3 | **literal soundId** (u8) — passed straight into the wrapper, which passes it straight to dispatcher `zz_00efb3c_` (bank = id>>7, sample = id&0x7f; battle banks snd_com01/02). u8 range covers banks 0-1 only. |

Modes (byte 2), chunk_0006.c:1836-1856:

- **0** — `zz_00f036c_(actor, id)`: play `id` positionally at actor+0x20 (world pos), through
  `zz_00f04b4_` -> `zz_00efb3c_(id, screenSide, vol, pan, 0x2000)` (chunk_0026.c:790-866).
- **1** — anim-rate pitch-variant select: reads `*(float*)(actor+0xb4)` (the anim playback
  rate) and plays `id-1` when rate < 0.7 (`FLOAT_80437134`), `id` when 0.7 <= rate <= 2.0
  (`FLOAT_80437138`), `id+1` when rate > 2.0 — i.e. the ROM stores the NORMAL-speed sample at
  `id` and expects slow/fast neighbors at id∓1. Then `zz_00f036c_` as mode 0.
- **2, 3** — `zz_00f061c_(0.2 /*FLOAT_8043713c*/, actor, id)`: position pulled 20% toward the
  local listener anchor before the positional play (chunk_0026.c:902-927); falls back to plain
  `zz_00f036c_` when the actor isn't on the listener's screen slot.
- **>= 4** — no-op (the `(bVar1 != 4) && (bVar1 < 4)` guard at chunk_0006.c:1854).

Evidence chain to the dispatcher: `zz_00f036c_` (chunk_0026.c:790) -> `zz_00efb3c_(id,0,0x7f,
0x40,0x2000)` when actorless, else `zz_00f04b4_` (chunk_0026.c:854) -> `zz_00efb3c_`.
`zz_00f061c_` (chunk_0026.c:902) -> `zz_00f04b4_`/`zz_00f036c_`. Same dispatcher already
proven for the 11 code-literal combat SE ids (scripts/export-combat-se.py header).

Timing: like every stream op, the record executes when the interpreter's wait clock reaches it
(op 0x00 wait records; `zz_004cd24_` chunk_0006.c:1996), so the preceding wait's frame context
IS the sound's play frame — identical semantics to how armHit frames were extracted.

**Usage census (honest negative):** walking EVERY slot of every known action-script bank
(the 114 distinct family/shared banks bound at actor+0x1d80/+0x1d84 across the 191 resolved
roster borgs — 12031 slots) finds **zero** op 0x0e records. Full op histogram:

rag_vectors.name: PATH B — the per-anim sound-event table at actor+0x4e8 (the REAL swing audio)
rag_vectors.text:
# [research/decomp/anim-sound-op-decode-2026-07-04.md] PATH B — the per-anim sound-event table at actor+0x4e8 (the REAL swing audio)
## PATH B — the per-anim sound-event table at actor+0x4e8 (the REAL swing audio)

Consumer: `zz_005b880_` (chunk_0007.c:3579-3624), called per frame from the actor anim
update. For each of the 4 anim parts (part state block = actor+0x1ad4 + part*0x40):

- `idx = *(s8*)(partState+0x37)`; -1 = this part's current anim has no sound events.
- events start at `*(actor+0x4e8) + idx*8` and are 8-byte records, list runs while the
  CURRENT record's byte+6 != 0xff (i.e. +6 is a continue/terminate flag).

Record layout (big-endian, consumed by `zz_005b98c_` chunk_0007.c:3632-3668):

| off | type | meaning |
|-----|------|---------|
| +0 | u16 | **frame** (anim-clock frame the sound fires on; 0 = anim start — sentinel compare vs `FLOAT_804373e4` = 0.0) |
| +2 | u8 | window flag: 0 = plain cur-frame compare, nonzero = windowed prev..cur compare (uses partState+0xc/+0x14 and s16 partState+0x24) |
| +3 | u8 | **mode** — IDENTICAL semantics to op 0x0e's byte2: 0 plain positional (`zz_00f036c_`), 1 anim-rate variant select id-1/id/id+1 (rate `actor+0xb4` vs 0.7/2.0 = `FLOAT_8043740c`/`FLOAT_80437410`), 2\|3 listener-lerped positional (`zz_00f061c_(0.2 /*FLOAT_80437404*/)`), >=4 no-op |
| +4 | u16 | **soundId** (u16 → full 0..0x17f dispatcher range; bank = id>>7, sample = id&0x7f) |
| +6 | u8 | continue flag (0xff = last record of this anim's list) |
| +7 | u8 | pad (0 in all sampled records) |

Where the pieces bind (all family-ctor stores, same recipe as the action-script banks):

- `actor+0x4e8` = the family's sound-event TABLE (e.g. G RED ctor `zz_018ccfc_`
  chunk_0046.c:4803: `PTR_DAT_8036556c` -> 0x80365878; the pl062a variant REBINDS it to
  0x803659a0 at chunk_0046.c:4813-4814 — per-borg ctor emulation is required, not per-family).
- `actor+0x1d88` / `+0x1d8c` = family/shared ANIM-DESCRIPTOR banks (chunk_0046.c:4810-4811;
  G RED 0x80367460 / 0x802bb698). Lookup `zz_004d1f4_` (chunk_0006.c:2210): same s16
  group-offset -> s16 slot-offset scheme as the action-script banks, keyed by the playAnim
  op's (group, slot) — group bit7 selects the shared bank. THIS is the join: the action
  stream plays anim (g, s); the descriptor record for (g, s) carries the sound-list index.
- Descriptor record (consumed by `zz_004d244_` chunk_0006.c:2232): per-part sub-records at
  +4/+0xc/+0x14/+0x1c; **byte +8+part*8 = the part's sound-list start index** into the
  +0x4e8 table (0xff = none). (Other descriptor bytes: +0 clip id -> partState+0x32,

rag_vectors.name: PATH B — the per-anim sound-event table at actor+0x4e8 (the REAL swing audio)
rag_vectors.text:
# [research/decomp/anim-sound-op-decode-2026-07-04.md] PATH B — the per-anim sound-event table at actor+0x4e8 (the REAL swing audio)
+4/+5 -> +0x2a/+0x2b, +6 flags, +7 -> u16 +0x26, part-0 extras +9/+0x11.. blend params.)

rag_vectors.name: Validation (G RED, pl0615)
rag_vectors.text:
# [research/decomp/anim-sound-op-decode-2026-07-04.md] Validation (G RED, pl0615)
### Validation (G RED, pl0615)

Every playAnim target of the family bank's group-3 (melee) hit-bearing streams resolves
through descriptor bank 0x80367460 to a well-formed 1-record sound list in table 0x80365878:
soundId **0x24** (heavy sword whoosh) or **0x0b** (light whoosh) at frames 4-21, windowFlag 1,
mode 0, cont 0xff, pad 0 — exactly a per-swing whoosh at windup, a few frames before each
kind's hit.bin active window. Shared-bank movement anims that carry no sounds read idx 0xff
(e.g. g0s14). Both ids resolve to audible TSB entries in snd_com01.

rag_vectors.name: Op 0x0f — table-driven voice/cry (id NOT in the operand)
rag_vectors.text:
# [research/decomp/anim-sound-op-decode-2026-07-04.md] Op 0x0f — table-driven voice/cry (id NOT in the operand)
## Op 0x0f — table-driven voice/cry (id NOT in the operand)

Handler `FUN_8004ca3c` (chunk_0006.c:1865-1877). 4-byte record: byte1 = part index (same
gate), byte2 must be 0, byte3 unused. Calls `zz_00b2190_(actor, 2)` (non-sound, deep-scan
clean — FX/state helper) then `zz_00f07e8_(actor)` (chunk_0026.c:972-987), which plays
`s16 DAT_80434410[v & 0x7f]` where `v = PTR_DAT_8031b7f4[*(char*)(actor+0x3e8)][*(u8*)(actor
+0x3e9)]` — i.e. a per-borg (family byte / index byte) VOICE table lookup; bit7 of the table
byte selects the listener-lerped wrapper `zz_00f061c_(0.2, ...)`. The sound id is **not**
extractable from the stream operand — it rides the borg-id-indexed table. Not wired this
session; the existing per-family voice-cue presentation (behavior-notes (az)) covers the
audible effect. Decoding the DAT_80434410/PTR_DAT_8031b7f4 tables is a separate follow-up.

rag_vectors.name: Extraction + port wiring (this session)
rag_vectors.text:
# [research/decomp/anim-sound-op-decode-2026-07-04.md] Extraction + port wiring (this session)
## Extraction + port wiring (this session)

- `scripts/gen-melee-anim-kinds.mjs`:
  - decodes stream op 0x0e into `{op:"playSound", ...}` events (emits nothing on GG4E — see
    the census above — but the decode is correct and future-proof);
  - ctor emulation now also captures `+0x1d88`/`+0x1d8c`/`+0x4e8`, and each stored stream's
    playAnim/blendAnim events are joined to their PATH-B sound lists; per-borg
    `borgs[id].animSounds["g{G}s{S}" | "shared:g{G}s{S}"] = [{frame, id, mode, part}]`
    (part-deduped by frame/id/mode — parts often share a list).
- `packages/combat/src/actionStreamData.ts` exposes the swing anim's sounds on
  ComboStep/ExactMoveLeaf (`sounds`); combat.ts hands the current swing's list to the
  renderer via `BorgRuntime.meleeSounds` (same bridge pattern as meleeAnimStream).
- `scripts/export-combat-se.py --export --ids <hex,...>` exports the census'd anim-event ids
  on top of the 11 code-literal ones.
- DERIVED: the id, its anim frame, and the sample audio. TUNED: the port plays the mode-1
  base id only (no anim-rate variant select — the port's anim rate is not the ROM's +0xb4
  clock), positional modes 0/2/3 all play flat, and the frame delay is scheduled off the
  slot-enter edge at 60fps rather than the ROM part-anim clock.

# [research/decomp/attack-mechanics-findings.md] Attack/combat mechanics audit — wiki taxonomy vs ROM evidence vs current port
# Attack/combat mechanics audit — wiki taxonomy vs ROM evidence vs current port

Generated by the Fable 5 attack-mechanics audit, 2026-07-03. Machine-readable twin:
`research/decomp/data/attack-mechanics-ledger.json`. Open questions:
`attack-mechanics-open-questions.md`. Dolphin plan: `attack-mechanics-trace-plan.md`.
Proposed port schema: `data/attack-profile-port-schema.json`. Cheap-agent tickets:
`research/tasks/attack-port/ATK-*.md`.

Confidence labels: `DERIVED_ROM`, `DERIVED_TRACE`, `CONFIRMED_ASSET`, `ASSET_INFERRED`,
`WIKI_TAXONOMY_ONLY`, `CURRENT_PORT`, `TUNED_EXISTING`, `FALLBACK_RECOMMENDED`, `BLOCKED`,
`CHECKED_CLOSED` (see the task brief / ledger `confidenceVocabulary`).

**Provenance note.** Citations marked "(corpus read, this audit)" come from single-pass
reads of `research/decomp/ghidra-export/chunk_*.c` performed by this audit's search agents.
Addresses/line numbers were reported with quoted decompiled code, but they have NOT been
re-verified by a second reader or a live trace — treat them as strong leads with exact
citations, one verification step below the notebook's Confirmed sections. Anything citing
`behavior-notes.md` sections (a)–(ah) inherits that notebook's stated confidence.

Wiki source: the Gotcha Force Wiki "Attacks" page (fandom). Direct fetch is blocked
(HTTP 402), so wiki claims below were reconstructed from search-result extracts of that page
plus the task brief's taxonomy. Wiki claims are a CHECKLIST, never numeric truth.

---

rag_vectors.name: Executive summary
rag_vectors.text:
# [research/decomp/attack-mechanics-findings.md] Executive summary
## Executive summary

- **The damage/stagger core is in good shape.** The full `zz_003cd5c_` damage formula is
  decoded and table-extracted (§ah), the gauge/stagger model is ported (gauges.ts), and —
  important negative result — the wiki's "hidden resistance/falloff" **is** the already-ported
  combo-rank falloff table `DAT_802c7ca0`. Nothing new to implement there; the risk is
  double-implementing it (see mechanic U).
- **Friendly fire is already ROM-correct in the formula and reachable in the sim loops** —
  same-team hits multiply by 0.25 unless record `flagsA & 0x1000`. What's missing is
  regression tests and the guard/block divisor wiring (mechanic V).
- **The biggest genuinely-new ROM findings of this audit**: (1) the attack-command decision
  layer (actor+0x585/+0x586 command/subtype bytes, decision testers in chunk_0009.c, command
  struct at actor+0x4b0 → actor+0x591 dispatch); (2) a complete ammo/refill system
  (3 weapon cells at +0x774/+0x77c/+0x784, refill types gradual/all-at-once/deployed-return,
  infinite-ammo rule); (3) a status-effect framework (status id +0x71a, timer +0x71c,
  immunity mask +0x5a0, shrink/grow +0x70c/+0x70e, param tier +0x74a); (4) Power Burst
  anchors (+0x6fb arm window, +0x6fc active flag: 2x out / 0.5x in / faster reload);
  (5) a real button-mash counter (+0x550, cap 4) in the attack-active handler.
- **The biggest blockers**: the physical-button → command mapping (the +0x5d4 word is a
  *transformed* button space; two corpus reads disagreed about which bit is which), the
  Power Burst meter/duration (no accumulator found in code — needs a RAM diff), the
  move → animation-bank → hitbox-record mapping (structurally decoded, per-move binding
  unknown), and projectile solidity (no generic proj-vs-proj code exists; must be per-type
  dispatch or absent).
- **What cheap agents can do now without guessing**: schema/shell work (command resolver
  types, weapon-cell ammo model, status runtime shell, burst state shell), regression tests
  for already-derived behavior (friendly fire, combo falloff, gauge stagger), and debug
  overlay fields. Everything context-numeric stays behind `BLOCKED until` gates.

rag_vectors.name: Current-port gap overview
rag_vectors.text:
# [research/decomp/attack-mechanics-findings.md] Current-port gap overview
## Current-port gap overview

| Area | Current port (CURRENT_PORT) | Gap vs ROM evidence |
|---|---|---|
| Attack input | `PlayerInput` has `jump/attack/special/lockOn/switchLock/allyLock/switchBorg/dash`; **no hyper/Y field** (types.ts:21-42) | ROM has a command vocabulary (type 1/2/3/5 + subtype 0-5) decided from buttons+state+distance, shared by player and CPU |
| B/X resolution | `stepAttacks` orders `["melee","shot"]` from `actionProfile.primary` (combat.ts:592-596); X = AoE special | ROM decides melee vs ranged per press via decision testers + distance/target gates, not a fixed per-borg primary |
| Melee contexts | One melee action + combo chain; no ground-dash/air/landing variants | ROM has command subtypes incl. grounded/air gates; full 8-context wiki list unproven |
| Projectiles | Spawn → home → first-hit consume (combat.ts:865-944); no proj-vs-proj, no penetration, no rehit | ROM: rehit interval (record +0x16 → object +0x4e), actor-gated despawn, flagsB collision gate; proj-vs-proj not found in generic code |
| Ammo | Single pool, refill-to-max after `reloadFrames` when empty (combat.ts:541-546) | ROM: 3 weapon cells, per-weapon refill type (gradual / all-at-once / deploy-return / special), shared rate boosted by burst, infinite when max==0 |
| Statuses | None (only `paramTier` fields exist, unconsumed) | ROM: status id/timer/immunity + shrink/grow + param tier with 900f timer |
| Hyper/Burst | Nothing | ROM: +0x6fb/+0x6fc/+0x6fa flags with 3 proven effects; meter/duration unknown |
| Mashing | Nothing | ROM: +0x550 press counter, cap 4, in slot-23 attack handler |
| Resistance/falloff | Ported (comboRankScale_802c7ca0 in damageFormula.ts:130-132) | None — do not add anything |
| Friendly fire | Ported in formula; teammates hittable in melee/special/projectile loops | Tests missing; block divisor (flagsA&0x1000 + victim 0x59c&0x1000 → /40) never activated by callers |

rag_vectors.text:
# [research/decomp/attack-mechanics-findings.md] Wiki taxonomy checklist
## Wiki taxonomy checklist

| # | Mechanic | Verdict this audit |
|---|---|---|
| A | B/X contextual attack resolution | Structure DERIVED_ROM; mapping BLOCKED (T1) |
| B | Melee priority at melee range | Partial (distance gates exist); exact rule BLOCKED (T1) |
| C | Ranged-only / melee-only borgs | ASSET_INFERRED + partial ROM (target gate, per-borg command table +0x4ec) |
| D–K | 8 melee movement contexts | WIKI_TAXONOMY_ONLY as a full list; subtype byte DERIVED_ROM (T2) |
| L | Passive contact/movement damage | WIKI_TAXONOMY_ONLY; FALLBACK_RECOMMENDED = disabled |
| M | Projectile solidity | WIKI_TAXONOMY_ONLY (no generic-code counterpart found) |
| N | Projectile-vs-projectile | Not found in generic collision; BLOCKED (per-type dispatch or T5) |
| O | Penetration | Mechanisms DERIVED_ROM (rehit/actor-gated despawn/flag gate); per-move values BLOCKED |
| P | Ammo/refill modes | DERIVED_ROM (mechanism complete); per-borg weapon-1/2 values need extraction |
| Q | Effects/statuses | Framework DERIVED_ROM; per-status semantics BLOCKED (T8) |
| R | Stagger/safety/super armor | Already ported (DERIVED_ROM); do-not-double-apply warnings below |
| S | Hyper Mode / Power Burst / Y | Flags+3 effects DERIVED_ROM; meter/duration/speed BLOCKED (T3) |
| T | Extra hits from mashing | DERIVED_ROM (counter + cap); which moves consume it BLOCKED |
| U | Resistance/damage falloff | RESOLVED: identical to ported comboRankScale_802c7ca0 |
| V | Friendly fire + OHKO exception | FF DERIVED_ROM & ported; ROM OHKO path CHECKED_CLOSED (none found) |
| W | Formula/combo/rank/FF interaction | Ported with documented defaulted contexts |
| X | Action/anim/hitbox mapping | Containers CONFIRMED_ASSET; per-move binding BLOCKED (T2) |

# [research/decomp/attack-mechanics-findings.md] Mechanic-by-mechanic findings

rag_vectors.name: A. B/X contextual attack resolution (`contextual_attack_resolution`)
rag_vectors.text:
# [research/decomp/attack-mechanics-findings.md] A. B/X contextual attack resolution (`contextual_attack_resolution`)
### A. B/X contextual attack resolution (`contextual_attack_resolution`)

- **wiki_taxonomy_claim**: B performs a context-dependent attack (melee when close/appropriate,
  ranged otherwise); X performs the secondary/special attack; some borgs' B and X swap roles
  or share ammo.
- **current_port_behavior**: `PlayerInput` carries `attack` (B, held bool) and `special` (X,
  pressed bool) only ([types.ts:21-42](../../packages/combat/src/types.ts)). `stepAttacks`
  resolves B by iterating `["melee","shot"]` ordered by `actionProfile.primary`
  ([combat.ts:592-635](../../packages/combat/src/combat.ts)) — a per-borg static preference,
  not per-press context; X is an instant AoE burst (combat.ts:549-589). No command
  abstraction shared with AI.
- **rom_or_asset_evidence** (corpus read, this audit, except where noted):
  - Command struct: actor+0x4b0 → pointer; bytes +0/+1 (buttons) and +4 (command) copied to
    actor+0x660/+0x661/+0x591 — `FUN_800562b8` chunk_0007.c:23 and `zz_006a8c0_` @0x8006a8c0
    chunk_0009.c:978-980.
  - Decision brain: `FUN_800699d8` @0x800699d8 chunk_0009.c:220 tests transformed button word
    actor+0x5d4 in priority order via testers `zz_0069cb0_` (→ +0x585=5, requires target:
    `if (actor+0x4a0 == 0) return 0;` chunk_0009.c:412), `zz_0069c50_` (bit 0x1000 → 5),
    `zz_0069bf0_` (bit 0x400 → 3, sets +0x595=1 "charged" mark), `zz_0069b98_` (bit 0x80 → 2),
    `zz_0069b10_` (bit 0x40 → 1), `zz_0069a88_` (bit 0x20 → 1, with grounded check).
  - Subtype: `zz_0069ea4_` chunk_0009.c:500-534 / `zz_0069fe0_` :554-587 set +0x586 ∈ 0-5
    from more button bits and state (value 4 = air/elevated; 5 = charged-ranged gated on
    `FLOAT_8043762c < actor+0x6a4` :555-556; a matching gate on +0x694 at :497-498).
  - Execution: `zz_006a104_` @0x8006a104 chunk_0009.c:603 looks up (type,subtype) through a
    per-actor table at +0x4ec and triggers the action.
  - Dispatch consumption: `FUN_8005d494` (attack-active state handler) branches +0x591==1/2
    (melee family) vs 4/5/6 (ranged family) — chunk_0007.c:4750-4782.
- **confidence**: DERIVED_ROM for the pipeline structure (decision testers → type/subtype →
  per-actor action table → state handler). BLOCKED for: (1) physical-button → +0x5d4 bit
  mapping (the word is transformed; the burst search read bit 0x200 as Y while the command
  search read 0x200 as B — at least one is wrong, both are guesses over a non-raw bit space),
  (2) the writer of the +0x4b0 command struct (not found in corpus — likely input/AI

rag_vectors.name: B. Melee priority over ranged at melee range (`melee_priority_at_range`)
rag_vectors.text:
# [research/decomp/attack-mechanics-findings.md] B. Melee priority over ranged at melee range (`melee_priority_at_range`)
### B. Melee priority over ranged at melee range (`melee_priority_at_range`)

- **wiki_taxonomy_claim**: at melee range, B produces the melee attack even for borgs that
  otherwise shoot.
- **current_port_behavior**: no distance check at all — `actionProfile.primary` fixes the
  order; a hybrid tries melee first only if `primary === "melee"` (combat.ts:592-609).
- **rom_or_asset_evidence**: the only distance gates found so far sit inside the
  subtype/tester functions (`FLOAT_8043762c` vs +0x694/+0x6a4, chunk_0009.c:497-498/555-556)
  and gate *charged-ranged* selection, not a general "melee wins up close" rule; ranged
  testers additionally require a live target (+0x4a0). The full priority rule is inside the
  not-yet-located command-struct writer / tester internals.
- **confidence**: partial DERIVED_ROM (distance-gated variant selection exists); BLOCKED for
  the actual B-at-melee-range rule.
- **exact_values_found**: none (threshold constant address known, value undumped).
- **unknowns**: whether melee-priority is distance-driven, target-state-driven, or per-borg.
- **likely_ts_touchpoints**: `commandResolver.ts` (once ATK-003 unblocks).
- **trace_needed**: T1 scripted distance sweep.
- **cheap_agent_ticket_id**: ATK-003.

rag_vectors.name: C. Ranged-only and melee-only behavior (`weapon_capability_gating`)
rag_vectors.text:
# [research/decomp/attack-mechanics-findings.md] C. Ranged-only and melee-only behavior (`weapon_capability_gating`)
### C. Ranged-only and melee-only behavior (`weapon_capability_gating`)

- **wiki_taxonomy_claim**: some borgs have no melee (pure gunners) or no ranged (pure melee);
  button presses fall through to whatever exists.
- **current_port_behavior**: `profile.hasMelee`/`hasShot` null out `melee`/`shot` defs and
  `primary` falls back accordingly (actionProfiles.ts:156-172) — ASSET-generated flags.
- **rom_or_asset_evidence**: per-actor command/action lookup table at +0x4ec consumed by
  `zz_006a104_` (chunk_0009.c:603+) — capability is data-driven per borg; ranged commands
  reject when no target (+0x4a0 gate); firing rejects when ammo short unless max==0
  (`zz_006dbe0_` @0x8006dbe0 chunk_0009.c:2876-2884; chunk_0002.c:7158-7165).
- **confidence**: ASSET_INFERRED (current flags) + DERIVED_ROM (gating mechanisms). The
  per-borg +0x4ec table's format is unread — BLOCKED for exact per-borg capabilities.
- **unknowns**: +0x4ec table layout and source file.
- **likely_ts_touchpoints**: actionProfiles generator, commandResolver.
- **trace_needed**: none first — corpus read of the +0x4ec initializer is the next step.
- **cheap_agent_ticket_id**: ATK-005 (mapping), research follow-up in open-questions Q3.

rag_vectors.name: D–K. Contextual melee variants (`contextual_melee_variants`)
rag_vectors.text:
# [research/decomp/attack-mechanics-findings.md] D–K. Contextual melee variants (`contextual_melee_variants`)
### D–K. Contextual melee variants (`contextual_melee_variants`)

standing (D), ground dash (E), air dash (F), flying (G), stabbing (H), jumping (I),
neutral air (J), landing (K).

- **wiki_taxonomy_claim** (per search extract + brief): melee differs by movement context —
  e.g. air-dash melee = B during/after an air dash; stabbing melee = B while airborne
  directly above an enemy; landing melee = B right after landing.
- **current_port_behavior**: exactly one melee action per borg (`MeleeActionDef`) with a
  combo chain; swings are startable from any non-busy state; no movement-context variants
  (combat.ts:598-609, actionProfiles.ts:20-35).
- **rom_or_asset_evidence**:
  - Asset side (CONFIRMED_ASSET, negative): animation banks are flat (group,slot) enums with
    NO movement-context axis — group 1 holds 5-23 generic `attack_s*` slots per borg;
    no `dash_attack`/`air_attack`/`landing` labels exist anywhere in the pipeline
    (`research/format-specs/borg-animation-banks.md`,
    `research/animation-actions/borg-action-assets.md`,
    `scripts/validate-borg-animation-actions.mjs:34-78`).
  - ROM side (corpus read, this audit): the command subtype byte +0x586 (0-5) IS a
    context/variant channel — value 4 is set on an air/elevated gate, testers apply grounded
    checks (chunk_0009.c:273-316, 500-534); +0x1d0f is a per-attack direction/variant
    selector 0-5 consumed in the attack handlers (chunk_0007.c:4259-4813); command values
    1 vs 2 are two distinct melee families.
  - Hitbox side (CONFIRMED_ASSET): each borg has up to 64 frame-windowed attack hitbox
    records selected via remap-B slots by spawner "kind" (`hit-bin-format.md`,
    `zz_008ac80_` @0x8008ac80) — enough authored data to back per-context hitboxes.
- **confidence**: DERIVED_ROM that a variant/context channel exists (type 1/2 + subtype 0-5 +
  +0x1d0f); WIKI_TAXONOMY_ONLY for the specific 8-context list and which context maps to
  which variant; CONFIRMED_ASSET for the hitbox/animation containers.
- **exact_values_found**: subtype range 0-5; variant selector range 0-5; per-borg attack-bank
  counts (e.g. pl0615: 23 group-1 slots).
- **unknowns**: context → (command,subtype) → (bank, remap slot) mapping.
- **likely_ts_touchpoints**: `types.ts` (movement-context enum on BorgRuntime),
  `movement.ts` (context capture: dashing/air/landing frames already computable),
  `commandResolver.ts`, `actionProfiles.ts` (per-context profile slots).

rag_vectors.name: L. Passive contact/movement damage (`passive_contact_damage`)
rag_vectors.text:
# [research/decomp/attack-mechanics-findings.md] L. Passive contact/movement damage (`passive_contact_damage`)
### L. Passive contact/movement damage (`passive_contact_damage`)

- **wiki_taxonomy_claim**: some borgs damage enemies by stepping on them, flying into them
  sword-out, air-dashing through them, or landing on them.
- **current_port_behavior**: none — damage only flows through melee swings, specials, and
  projectiles.
- **rom_or_asset_evidence**: none found. The generic collision passes resolve hitbox records
  with active frame windows; body records (0x020-0xA1f region) are persistent HURTboxes
  (activeEnd=-1). A persistent per-move ATTACK record (activeEnd=-1 in the attack region)
  would be a plausible carrier (the format allows it), but no such record use was confirmed —
  ASSET_INFERRED possibility only. No movement-handler damage side effects found in the
  state-handler reads (behavior-notes.md §u read every handler body).
- **confidence**: WIKI_TAXONOMY_ONLY. FALLBACK_RECOMMENDED: disabled (no contact damage)
  until a trace shows it.
- **unknowns**: whether contact damage is authored as persistent attack hitboxes tied to
  movement states, and its per-target re-hit pacing (the rehit-interval mechanism would
  cover it if so).
- **likely_ts_touchpoints**: none yet (scaffolding only).
- **trace_needed**: extend T2 with a "walk into enemy / land on enemy" script for a stomper
  borg (wiki names heavy/giant borgs).
- **cheap_agent_ticket_id**: ATK-006 (disabled-by-default scaffolding).

rag_vectors.name: M. Projectile solidity (`projectile_solidity`)
rag_vectors.text:
# [research/decomp/attack-mechanics-findings.md] M. Projectile solidity (`projectile_solidity`)
### M. Projectile solidity (`projectile_solidity`)

- **wiki_taxonomy_claim** (search extract): solid projectiles are physical objects
  (missiles, drills); nonsolid are energy; solids can be destroyed mid-air by melee, other
  solids, or nonsolids; nonsolids pass through each other.
- **current_port_behavior**: projectiles never interact with each other or with melee —
  only borg hit tests (combat.ts:901-941).
- **rom_or_asset_evidence**: NOT found in the generic collision code. The three collision
  pair passes (`collision_hit_pair_pass_active_vs_borgs` @0x8002da88 chunk_0003.c:7031,
  `..._active_vs_secondary` @0x8002db58 :7173) pair object lists without a solidity flag;
  no proj-vs-proj loop exists there. If solidity exists it lives in per-object-type update
  dispatch (`PTR_FUN_802da740` table, chunk_0013.c:1172) — unread. Visual family data
  (`projectileVisualFamilies.json`) is explicitly visual-only (behavior-notes §t) — do NOT
  infer solidity from it (that would be ASSET_INFERRED at best).
- **confidence**: WIKI_TAXONOMY_ONLY; the generic-path negative is a corpus-read negative,
  not CHECKED_CLOSED (per-type dispatch bodies unread).
- **unknowns**: candidate solidity flag; which object types participate.
- **likely_ts_touchpoints**: none until evidence (schema field reserved in
  `attack-profile-port-schema.json`).
- **trace_needed**: T5.
- **cheap_agent_ticket_id**: ATK-007 (blocked until T5 or dispatch read).

rag_vectors.name: O. Penetration (`penetration`)
rag_vectors.text:
# [research/decomp/attack-mechanics-findings.md] O. Penetration (`penetration`)
### O. Penetration (`penetration`)

- **wiki_taxonomy_claim**: certain attacks penetrate certain objects; melee and explosions
  effectively have total penetration.
- **current_port_behavior**: every projectile is consumed by its first valid borg hit
  (`consumed = true; break;` combat.ts:936-939). Melee already "penetrates" trivially
  (per-swing hit list `meleeHitUids`, one hit per target per swing, combat.ts:654-692).
- **rom_or_asset_evidence** (corpus read, this audit):
  - No destroy-projectile-on-hit exists in the collision/hit-resolution code; object death is
    `object+0x10 = 2` set by actor-gated logic (chunk_0013.c:1188, gated on an actor flag
    +0x281), i.e. persistence-after-hit is the structural default and despawn is per-type.
  - Re-hit pacing: damage record s8+0x16 (`rehitIntervalFrames`) reloads a per-OBJECT counter
    at object+0x4e; on expiry the hit re-applies (chunk_0013.c:1175-1182). Counter is global
    to the object, not per-target.
  - Collision gate: hit allowed iff `(attackerRec.flagsB & 0x4000)==0 ||
    (targetRec.flagsB & 0x2000)!=0` (chunk_0003.c:7080). NOTE: flagsB bit 0x4000 is ALSO the
    skip-combo-falloff bit in the damage formula (§ah step 16) — one bit observed in two
    consumers; do not treat the collision-gate reading as settled semantics.
  - All 9 extracted borg-family damage records have `rehitIntervalFrames = 0` and flagsB = 0
    (`packages/combat/src/data/damageRecords.json`); the interesting values live in the
    generic/comhit table `DAT_802c4760` and per-move hit-bin data (partially extracted:
    `research/decomp/data/damage-records-802d46e0.json` covers both tables' bytes).
- **confidence**: DERIVED_ROM (mechanisms: rehit counter, actor-gated despawn, flag gate);
  BLOCKED (per-move values and true flag semantics).
- **exact_values_found**: gate expression above; record field offsets.
- **unknowns**: which moves persist through borgs; explosion spawn-on-impact (no effect-id
  consumer found in the generic hit path — corpus-read negative).
- **likely_ts_touchpoints**: `types.ts` `Projectile` (penetration/rehit fields),
  `combat.ts` `stepProjectiles`.
- **trace_needed**: T6.
- **cheap_agent_ticket_id**: ATK-008 (consumption refactor — blocked until flag mapping/T6).

rag_vectors.name: P. Ammo / refill modes (`ammo_refill`)
rag_vectors.text:
# [research/decomp/attack-mechanics-findings.md] P. Ammo / refill modes (`ammo_refill`)
### P. Ammo / refill modes (`ammo_refill`)

- **wiki_taxonomy_claim** (search extract): two refill families — gradual (starts refilling
  right after use, one shot at a time) and all-at-once (won't start until fully depleted);
  plus deployed weapons that return; Hyper Mode reloads instantly.
- **current_port_behavior**: one ammo pool; when `ammo <= 0` and no `reload` cooldown, refill
  to `ammoMax` (combat.ts:541-546); firing decrements and arms `reload = reloadFrames` when
  empty (combat.ts:738-745). `ammoMax`/`reloadFrames` are TUNED_EXISTING profile values.
- **rom_or_asset_evidence** (corpus read, this audit — the strongest new system decode):
  - 3 weapon cells stride 8: cur s16 +0x774/+0x77c/+0x784, prev s16 +0x776/…, refill timer
    f32 +0x778/…; aux cells stride 8 from +0x78c: max s16 +0x78e/…, refill type s16
    +0x790/…, refill param s16 +0x792/… . Init from the same stat row as HP
    (chunk_0007.c:60-94; row source `zz_0055f90_` §ag — weapon-0 values already extracted to
    `research/decomp/data/borg-hp-stat-rows-802f2988.json`, live-verified G RED ammo 5).
  - Fire gate `zz_006dbe0_(actor, weaponIdx, cost, actuallyFire)` @0x8006dbe0
    chunk_0009.c:2866-2905: blocks if cur < cost; dry-run mode; decrement writes prev=cur.
    65+ call sites.
  - Per-frame refill `zz_006dcc0_` @0x8006dcc0 chunk_0009.c:2909-2973: rate float from
    +0x768, multiplied by FLOAT_804376c8 when +0x6fc (burst) set; per-weapon disable bits
    +0x597; type 1 = gradual (fractional grant toward max), type 0/negative = all-at-once on
    timer expiry; types 2/3 initialize a constant timer (semantics unread).
  - One-shot grant `zz_006de10_(actor, weaponIdx)` @0x8006de10 (cur+=1 clamp max) — the
    deployed-object-return path (chunk_0029.c:2270-2301 restores weapon-2 ammo when a deploy
    fails/dies; more in chunk_0036.c).
  - Infinite-ammo rule: gate skipped when max==0 (chunk_0002.c:7158-7165).
- **confidence**: DERIVED_ROM (mechanism end-to-end). Values: weapon-0 per-borg values
  extracted; weapon-1/2 rows and the +0x768 rate's init site still open. One field conflict:
  a parallel status-effect read claimed +0x768 is a scale-related float — see open-questions
  Q7 before consuming +0x768.
- **exact_values_found**: offsets above; refill-type enum {≤0 all-at-once, 1 gradual, 2-3
  special}; burst boosts refill; infinite when max==0.
- **likely_ts_touchpoints**: `types.ts` (BorgRuntime.ammo → weaponCells[3]),

rag_vectors.name: Q. Effects / statuses (`status_effects`)
rag_vectors.text:
# [research/decomp/attack-mechanics-findings.md] Q. Effects / statuses (`status_effects`)
### Q. Effects / statuses (`status_effects`)

- **wiki_taxonomy_claim**: attacks inflict statuses — freeze/shock/stun, poison, shrink/grow,
  pull/tether, barrier/block (blocks enemy-team projectiles), etc.
- **current_port_behavior**: none. `BorgRuntime.paramTier` fields exist but nothing consumes
  them ([types.ts:73-80,133-134](../../packages/combat/src/types.ts)).
- **rom_or_asset_evidence** (corpus read, this audit):
  - Status id: victim+0x71a = hitboxRecord[1] & 0x3f (chunk_0003.c:7641-7642); duration
    victim+0x71c = max-merge from record[4] (:7638-7639), -1/frame (chunk_0007.c:2881-2883).
  - Immunity: victim+0x5a0 (u32; bit N blocks status N AND zeroes HP damage for that hit —
    formula step 1, chunk_0004.c:6693-6699); init zeroed (chunk_0006.c:7107-7110); per-borg
    immunities presumably from pl data (init site unfound).
  - Status-blocked flag: 0x5e0 & 0x20000000 (chunk_0003.c:7644-7649).
  - Shrink victim+0x70c (gate puVar17[9]&0x400, keeps max duration, chunk_0003.c:7673-7676);
    grow victim+0x70e (gate puVar17[9]&0x800, :8105-8107); both scale via LUT DAT_802dd57c
    applied at chunk_0007.c:2868-2876.
  - Param tier: +0x74a tier / +0x74e accum / +0x750 timer=900; tier rows PTR_DAT_804339e0
    feed actor+0xb8/+0x1dd0/+0x5f8 (chunk_0008.c:4396-4519) — a 15-second buff/debuff system.
  - Negatives: no DoT HP-drain loop found; no OHKO path (damage floors at 1,
    chunk_0004.c:6818-6821); no separate freeze input-lock flag found beyond the reaction
    system.
- **confidence**: DERIVED_ROM (framework fields); BLOCKED (per-id semantics, durations,
  which moves inflict what — hit-bin record bytes [1]/[4]/[5] carry them per move).
- **likely_ts_touchpoints**: `types.ts` (status fields on BorgRuntime), new
  `packages/combat/src/status.ts` shell, `combat.ts` applyHit + a per-frame stepStatus.
- **trace_needed**: T8.
- **cheap_agent_ticket_id**: ATK-010 (runtime shell, no behavior).

rag_vectors.name: R. Stagger / safety / super armor (`stagger_safety_model`)
rag_vectors.text:
# [research/decomp/attack-mechanics-findings.md] R. Stagger / safety / super armor (`stagger_safety_model`)
### R. Stagger / safety / super armor (`stagger_safety_model`)

- **wiki_taxonomy_claim** (search extract): attacks carry values controlling how fast a hit
  borg staggers or enters "invulnerable safety".
- **current_port_behavior**: ALREADY PORTED, gauge-based (gauges.ts; applyHit
  combat.ts:276-345): down-gauge depletion / balance break / record reaction-flag force;
  balance break grants STAGGER.STAGGER_IFRAMES (60, balance-break only); post-hit
  60-frame windows; combo accumulator/rank; wake invincibility 60.
- **rom_or_asset_evidence**: behavior-notes §ag/§ah/§s + gauges.ts provenance blocks — the
  wiki's "stagger values" are the damage record's `downGaugeDamage`/`balanceGaugeDamage`/
  `reactionFlags`; "safety" is the balance-break stagger + i-frame grant and wake
  invincibility; "super armor" is high per-borg gauge bases (tanks/bosses in
  gaugeInit.json), not a separate flag. The 0x5e0 & 0x4000000 half-damage bit is a defensive
  buff state, not armor (formula step 17).
- **confidence**: DERIVED_ROM + CURRENT_PORT (match).
- **DO NOT DOUBLE-APPLY**:
  1. Do not add per-hit flat hitstun — the ROM has none (gauges.ts header;
     chunk_0003.c:6255-6263). MELEE.HITSTUN is only the TUNED reaction LENGTH after a
     confirmed stagger.
  2. Do not add a "super armor" flag — model it via gauge bases already loaded per borg.
  3. Do not grant stagger i-frames on non-balance-break staggers (combat.ts:327-335 comment).
  4. Reaction length stays animation-gated/TUNED (constants.ts header) — porting 60 as
     hitstun remains forbidden (§s).
- **cheap_agent_ticket_id**: none needed beyond regression coverage folded into ATK-014's
  test pattern; open verification lead in T4/T9 (reaction lengths).

rag_vectors.name: S. Hyper Mode / Power Burst / Y (`hyper_mode`)
rag_vectors.text:
# [research/decomp/attack-mechanics-findings.md] S. Hyper Mode / Power Burst / Y (`hyper_mode`)
### S. Hyper Mode / Power Burst / Y (`hyper_mode`)

- **wiki_taxonomy_claim** (search extract): Power Burst grants instantly-reloading ammo,
  massive speed, and much higher damage; activated with Y when the meter allows; co-op sync
  and fused-borg interactions exist.
- **current_port_behavior**: nothing — no Y field on PlayerInput, no meter, no burst flag.
- **rom_or_asset_evidence** (corpus read, this audit):
  - Arm gate: `FUN_80069814` chunk_0009.c:104-115 — `if ((actor+0x5d4 & 0x200)==0) return 0;
    actor+0x6fb = 6;` (a 6-frame arm window; bit identity in the transformed word unproven).
  - Consume: `zz_005b2b8_` @0x8005b2b8 chunk_0007.c:3354-3494 — requires +0x6fb != 0 and
    active borg; pair-match attempt via `zz_005b678_` (species pair table 0x802d352c,
    chunk_0007.c:3500-3553); sets +0x6fc = 1 and +0x6fa = 1 (chunk_0007.c:3439-3441); paired
    path syncs headings between two actors (chunk_0007.c:3192-3198) — the two-player/fusion
    hook.
  - Proven +0x6fc effects: outgoing damage x2.0 / incoming x0.5 (formula steps 3/10, §ah,
    chunk_0004.c:6705-6707/6761-6763); ammo refill rate x FLOAT_804376c8 (`zz_006dcc0_`,
    chunk_0009.c:2923-2927) — matching the wiki's "instantly-reloading ammo" directionally.
  - Negatives (corpus read): NO meter accumulator found anywhere in the damage path; NO
    +0x6fc-gated movement/attack-speed modifier found; NO explicit +0x6fc clear/duration
    (only +0x6fb clears, chunk_0007.c:3922). NO "hyper/burst" strings in the DOL.
- **meter/duration RESOLVED (2026-07-03, save-state diff via scripts/diff-actor-block.mjs
  + targeted corpus re-read)**: the meter lives in the PER-PLAYER struct at
  `PTR_DAT_80433934` (= 0x803c5420 in GG4E, static across boots), stride 0x3c per player:
  - `+0x3c*i + 0x126` (u16): **meter value** (verified live: 2929 in "nexthitChargesMeter"
    save -> 3000 in "meter charged" save);
  - `+0x3c*i + 0x124` (u16): **meter max = 3000** (init chunk_0000.c:1128-1131, together
    with meter=0 and +0x12a=0);
  - `+0x3c*i + 0x12a` (u16): unclamped companion accumulator (2929 -> 3255 across the same
    pair — keeps counting past max while +0x126 clamps);
  - `+0x3c*i + 0x103` (u8): **charged/ready flag** (0 -> 1 in the same diff) — this is the
    activation precondition: `FUN_80069814` refuses to arm unless it is nonzero
    (chunk_0009.c:107), answering the "arm gate precondition" unknown;
  - `+0x3c*i + 0x104` (u8): burst-active mode (1 = Y-activated burst; 2 = spawn-granted

  Ported: BURST.DRAIN_PER_FRAME / BURST.SPEED_MULTIPLIER (constants.ts).

  from two angles — Power Burst is the player-facing name; the pair/fusion path is its

# [research/decomp/attack-mechanics-findings.md] T. Extra hits from mashing / stick movement (`mash_extra_hits`)
### T. Extra hits from mashing / stick movement (`mash_extra_hits`)

- **wiki_taxonomy_claim**: mashing buttons (or rotating the stick) during certain multi-hit
  attacks adds hits.
- **current_port_behavior**: none.
- **rom_or_asset_evidence** (corpus read, this audit): in attack-active handler slot 23
  (`FUN_8005d494`): `if (actor+0x1d10 == 1) actor+0x550 += 1;` (chunk_0007.c:4809-4810);
  phase advances when `4 < actor+0x550` or +0x552 != 0 (:4816) — a press counter with cap 4.
  Press-edge derivation: +0x5bc = +0x5b4 & ~+0x5b8 (chunk_0007.c:291). Stick rotation: only
  a binary out-of-deadzone flag (+0x6fd & 0x40) affecting direction/variant selection —
  no rotation accumulator found (corpus-read negative).

  an extra "hit" does downstream). FALLBACK_RECOMMENDED: disabled.
- **exact_values_found**: cap constant 4 (comparison `4 < +0x550`).
- **likely_ts_touchpoints**: `combat.ts` melee-active block (future), `types.ts` cooldowns.

rag_vectors.name: U. Resistance / damage falloff (`resistance_falloff`) — HIGHEST-RISK ITEM, RESOLVED
rag_vectors.text:
# [research/decomp/attack-mechanics-findings.md] U. Resistance / damage falloff (`resistance_falloff`) — HIGHEST-RISK ITEM, RESOLVED
### U. Resistance / damage falloff (`resistance_falloff`) — HIGHEST-RISK ITEM, RESOLVED

- **wiki_taxonomy_claim**: repeated hits on the same target do less and less damage (hidden
  resistance that recovers over time).
- **current_port_behavior**: `computeSourceDamage` applies
  `dmg *= comboRankScale_802c7ca0[victim.comboRank]` unless `record.flagsB & 0x4000`
  (damageFormula.ts:130-132); `comboAccum`/`comboRank` grow per hit (applyHit,
  combat.ts:306-311) and reset when the 60-frame combo window expires
  (stepGaugeWindows, combat.ts:371-378).
- **rom_or_asset_evidence**: formula step 16 (§ah, chunk_0004.c:6804-6806):
  `dmg *= DAT_802c7ca0[victim+0x6ca]` = [1.0, 0.8, 0.5, 0.25, 0.1, 0.05, 0.02, …];
  accumulator/rank mechanics chunk_0003.c:8021-8030; window reset chunk_0006.c:8009-8010.
- **confidence**: DERIVED_ROM, ported. **Verdict: the wiki mechanic IS
  comboRankScale_802c7ca0 — exactly, not partially.**
- **double_application_risk**: YES if anyone adds a new "resistance" layer. **Instruction to
  all agents: do not implement any additional per-target damage-decay mechanic.** The only
  legitimate follow-ups are tests and the flagsB&0x4000 exemption already coded.
- **cheap_agent_ticket_id**: ATK-013 (audit note + pin-down tests only).

rag_vectors.name: V. Friendly fire and OHKO exception (`friendly_fire`)
rag_vectors.text:
# [research/decomp/attack-mechanics-findings.md] V. Friendly fire and OHKO exception (`friendly_fire`)
### V. Friendly fire and OHKO exception (`friendly_fire`)

- **wiki_taxonomy_claim**: teammates take reduced damage from each other; one-hit-KO attacks
  ignore the reduction.
- **current_port_behavior**: `computeSourceDamage` multiplies by 0.25
  (`sameTeam_80437024`) when `attacker.team === victim.team` and `(record.flagsA & 0x1000)
  === 0` (damageFormula.ts:133-135). Teammates ARE hittable: melee/special loops use
  `canReceiveHit` (self-excluded only, combat.ts:118-120,560,655) and projectiles hit any
  non-owner (combat.ts:903). The /40 block divisor exists but no caller sets
  `blockDivisorActive` (damageFormula.ts:136-138).
- **rom_or_asset_evidence**: formula step 18 (§ah, chunk_0004.c:6811-6817): same team
  (+0x88 match) && !(flagsA & 0x1000) → x0.25 (FLOAT_80437024); (flagsA & 0x1000) &&
  victim+0x59c & 0x1000 → /40 (FLOAT_80437028); floor at 1 → int. Victim +0x59c comes from
  pl####data.bin u16@0xa8 (§ag).
- **confidence**: DERIVED_ROM, ported. OHKO: **CHECKED_CLOSED for the damage formula** — no
  damage=maxHP or instant-kill path exists in `zz_003cd5c_` or the hit path (searches:
  status/OHKO agent sweep of chunk_0004.c formula + chunk_0003.c hit resolution; damage
  floors at 1). The wiki's "OHKO" is most plausibly just moves whose hpDamage exceeds any
  HP pool (WIKI_TAXONOMY_ONLY), with flagsA&0x1000 as the FF-exemption channel.
- **unknowns**: which real moves carry flagsA&0x1000 (per-move hit-bin data).
- **cheap_agent_ticket_id**: ATK-014 (regression tests; no behavior change).

# [research/decomp/attack-mechanics-findings.md] Top blockers for 1:1 combat (ranked)
## Top blockers for 1:1 combat (ranked)

1. **Physical button → command mapping + command-struct writer** (mechanic A/B; trace T1).
   Everything contextual hangs off this.
2. **Move → animation bank → hitbox remap slot binding** (mechanics D-K/X; trace T2 + corpus
   read of `zz_008ac80_` "kind" producers). Gates authored hitboxes and per-move damage
   records replacing the 3-archetype mapping in gauges.ts.
3. **Power Burst meter + duration** (mechanic S; trace T3 RAM diff).
4. **Knockback magnitude / reaction lengths** (pre-existing; trace T9) — still the largest
   feel gap in already-ported systems.
5. **Projectile solidity / per-type dispatch** (mechanics M/N/O; trace T5/T6 + reading
   PTR_FUN_802da740 handler bodies).

rag_vectors.name: Q1. What writes the per-actor command struct (+0x4b0), and which physical button maps to which +0x5d4 bit?
rag_vectors.text:
# [research/decomp/attack-mechanics-open-questions.md] Q1. What writes the per-actor command struct (+0x4b0), and which physical button maps to which +0x5d4 bit?
## Q1. What writes the per-actor command struct (+0x4b0), and which physical button maps to which +0x5d4 bit?

- **Why it matters**: this is the whole B/X contextual-attack resolver. Until the writer and
  the bit mapping are pinned, every command mapping in the port is a guess. Two independent
  corpus reads already produced CONFLICTING guesses for bit 0x200 (B vs Y) — proof that the
  +0x5d4 word is a transformed space that cannot be resolved by reading alone.
- **Evidence searched**: PAD/SI symbols (SDK wrappers only, chunk_0063/0065); all writers of
  actor+0x4b0; stores of small constants near button-bit tests in chunk_0009.c; CPU-AI index
  (`research/decomp/index/cpu-ai-evidence.md`). The decision testers and +0x585/+0x586
  outputs ARE found (chunk_0009.c:220-587); the struct writer is not.
- **Next search/trace**: (a) grep for writers to the memory the +0x4b0 pointers target —
  start from where +0x4b0 itself is initialized (find `0x4b0) =` stores and the allocation
  site); (b) otherwise go straight to Dolphin trace T1: watch commandStruct bytes while
  pressing scripted buttons — 30 minutes of tracing beats another corpus day here.
- **Expected output**: table physical-button -> +0x5d4 bit -> command value, plus the
  writer function address; appended to behavior-notes.md as a new lettered section.
- **Agent type**: grep (one more targeted pass), then **Dolphin** (T1). NOT a coding task.

rag_vectors.name: Q4. Power Burst meter: which field accumulates, what threshold arms activation, and what clears +0x6fc (duration)? — MOSTLY RESOLVED (2026-07-03)
rag_vectors.text:
# [research/decomp/attack-mechanics-open-questions.md] Q4. Power Burst meter: which field accumulates, what threshold arms activation, and what clears +0x6fc (duration)? — MOSTLY RESOLVED (2026-07-03)
## Q4. Power Burst meter: which field accumulates, what threshold arms activation, and what clears +0x6fc (duration)? — MOSTLY RESOLVED (2026-07-03)

- **Resolved by save-state diff (scripts/diff-actor-block.mjs) + targeted corpus re-read**
  (full detail in attack-mechanics-findings.md §S): meter = per-player struct
  `PTR_DAT_80433934 + i*0x3c + 0x126` (u16), max at +0x124 = 3000, charged flag +0x103
  (the FUN_80069814 arm precondition), burst mode +0x104 (1=Y burst, 2=spawn hyper),
  duration countdown +0x10c/+0x10e = 600 frames (Y) / 1800 frames (spawn). +0x6fc clear:
  zz_005b2b8_ top-of-frame sweep when the player's +0x104 drops to 0.
- **FILL RULE RESOLVED (2026-07-03 T3 live traces)**: **+50 per hit connection, attacker
  only, flat** — independent of damage dealt:
  - B shot on live G Red: 16 HP damage -> +50 meter (reproduced 3x);
  - Plasma Knuckle melee on live G Red: 25 HP damage -> +50 meter;
  - one penetrating beam through a DEAD borg husk: +150 = 3 hit connections x 50
    (dead borgs still register hits; G Red beam penetration = "borgs");
  - victim side: NONE of the other three players' meters moved on any hit taken —
    taking damage does NOT fill the victim's meter;
  - clamp/flag timing (live): +0x126 clamps at max 3000 while +0x12a keeps accumulating
    (2929 -> 3079 -> 3129); charged flag +0x103 flips to 1 ONE frame after +0x126
    reaches max.
  - Caveat: 50 confirmed for two different G Red moves (shot + melee); a per-move or
    per-record override elsewhere can't be fully excluded. NOTE the damage-record field
    `forceGaugeCurveIndex` is NOT this — that's a damage multiplier keyed to the TEAM
    GF-energy gauge (player struct +0x114 max), a separate gauge from the burst circle
    (+0x124/+0x126). The damage pipeline (chunk_0004.c:6672-6828) never writes +0x126.
- **Remaining (minor)**: live-verify the 600-frame duration + what the +0x12a unclamped
  accumulator feeds (results screen? chunk_0022.c:1671/1737 reads it for min/max across
  players); Q5 speed boost (non-burst baseline captured: 22.0 world-units/frame dash).
- ATK-012 fill rule: **unblocked** — port as `onHitConnect(attacker): meter = min(meter+50,
  3000); unclamped += 50; if (meter == 3000) charged = 1` per player.

rag_vectors.name: Q5. Where does Power Burst's speed boost live (wiki: "massive speed"), given no +0x6fc-gated speed modifier exists? — RESOLVED AT VALUE LEVEL (2026-07-04)
rag_vectors.text:
# [research/decomp/attack-mechanics-open-questions.md] Q5. Where does Power Burst's speed boost live (wiki: "massive speed"), given no +0x6fc-gated speed modifier exists? — RESOLVED AT VALUE LEVEL (2026-07-04)
## Q5. Where does Power Burst's speed boost live (wiki: "massive speed"), given no +0x6fc-gated speed modifier exists? — RESOLVED AT VALUE LEVEL (2026-07-04)

- **Live-measured (T3-q5-speed-final.jsonl, live paired activation with owner at both pads)**:
  **burst movement speed = x1.5** — median moving delta 33.0 world-units/frame during burst vs
  the proven 22.0 u/f ground-walk baseline ((ac)). Ported as BURST.SPEED_MULTIPLIER.
- **Duration model CORRECTED by the same trace**: the +0x10c/+0x10e=600 timer runs in parallel
  with the METER ITSELF draining -5/frame from 3000 (BURST.DRAIN_PER_FRAME; 343 clean -5
  frames measured; 3000/5 = the 600f ceiling). Burst ends when the meter empties — observed
  437 frames because ACTIONS SPEND EXTRA meter: discrete -45/-50/-60 (dash-shaped) and
  -350/-470 events; the drain ledger sums to exactly 3000. Also confirmed: versus-mode
  activation requires BOTH teammates armed within the 6-frame window (zz_005b2b8_ candidate
  count > 1 unless player flag +0xf5 == 1) — solo Y just arms and expires.
- **Still open (minor)**: the code path carrying the x1.5 (no +0x6fc-gated modifier in the
  read corpus; moveMult1dd4/param-tier fields did NOT change at activation — likely inside
  the movement-physics bucket), and the per-action meter-cost table.

  per-object-type dispatch table PTR_FUN_802da740 (chunk_0013.c:1172) bodies are UNREAD.
- **Next search/trace**: enumerate PTR_FUN_802da740's pointer targets (dol.py raw read, same
  technique as the 35-slot state table) and skim each handler for object-object hit tests;
  in parallel, Dolphin T5 head-on projectile tests.
- **Expected output**: either the solidity flag/field, or a corpus-wide CHECKED_CLOSED.
- **Agent type**: grep + dol.py (table dump), then Dolphin T5.

rag_vectors.name: Q7. What is actor+0x768 really — ammo refill rate, scale-related float, or both consumers misread?
rag_vectors.text:
# [research/decomp/attack-mechanics-open-questions.md] Q7. What is actor+0x768 really — ammo refill rate, scale-related float, or both consumers misread?
## Q7. What is actor+0x768 really — ammo refill rate, scale-related float, or both consumers misread?

- **Why it matters**: ATK-009's refill model reads +0x768 as the rate; the status search
  read the same offset as a scale-reduction float multiplied by shrink/grow LUTs. Both were
  single-pass corpus reads; at least one is wrong (or the field is genuinely shared, which
  would itself matter).
- **Evidence searched**: zz_006dcc0_ (chunk_0009.c:2923) vs chunk_0007.c:2868-2876.
- **Next search/trace**: re-read both sites carefully (they may be different structs — the
  chunk_0007 site may be on a different object type); confirm the struct identity via the
  surrounding field usage; T7/T8 logging settles it live.
- **Expected output**: one confirmed role per struct; corrects the findings/ledger.
- **Agent type**: grep (careful re-read), then Dolphin if still ambiguous.

rag_vectors.name: Q8. Which status id (0-63) maps to which player-visible effect, and how is poison implemented (no DoT loop found)?
rag_vectors.text:
# [research/decomp/attack-mechanics-open-questions.md] Q8. Which status id (0-63) maps to which player-visible effect, and how is poison implemented (no DoT loop found)?
## Q8. Which status id (0-63) maps to which player-visible effect, and how is poison implemented (no DoT loop found)?

- **Why it matters**: mechanic Q can only ship as a shell until ids mean something.
- **Evidence searched**: id/timer/immunity plumbing found; per-id consumers beyond
  shrink/grow not isolated; no periodic HP drain anywhere.
- **Next search/trace**: grep readers of victim+0x71a outside the application site (reaction
  selection? per-id branch table?); Dolphin T8 for ground truth.
- **Expected output**: status-id table with effects + durations -> turns ATK-010's shell
  into real behavior tickets.
- **Agent type**: grep first, **Dolphin** (T8) for semantics.

rag_vectors.name: Q9. What consumes the mash counter +0x550 past the cap-4 comparison — extra hitbox activations, duration extension, or something else?
rag_vectors.text:
# [research/decomp/attack-mechanics-open-questions.md] Q9. What consumes the mash counter +0x550 past the cap-4 comparison — extra hitbox activations, duration extension, or something else?
## Q9. What consumes the mash counter +0x550 past the cap-4 comparison — extra hitbox activations, duration extension, or something else?

- **Why it matters**: mechanic T; determines whether mashing adds hits (wiki) or advances
  phases faster.
- **Evidence searched**: increment + cap in FUN_8005d494 (chunk_0007.c:4809-4816); +0x552
  companion flag; no other consumers checked yet.
- **Next search/trace**: grep all readers of +0x550/+0x552 corpus-wide; then T2 mash logging.
- **Expected output**: the downstream effect -> spec for ATK-017's real behavior.
- **Agent type**: grep, then Dolphin.

rag_vectors.name: Q10. Which real moves set damage-record flagsA bit 0x1000 (friendly-fire exemption / guard interaction) and flagsB bits 0x4000/0x2000?
rag_vectors.text:
# [research/decomp/attack-mechanics-open-questions.md] Q10. Which real moves set damage-record flagsA bit 0x1000 (friendly-fire exemption / guard interaction) and flagsB bits 0x4000/0x2000?
## Q10. Which real moves set damage-record flagsA bit 0x1000 (friendly-fire exemption / guard interaction) and flagsB bits 0x4000/0x2000?

- **Why it matters**: FF exception (V), penetration gate (O), and combo-falloff exemption (U)
  all key off these bits; per-move values live in hit-bin/comhit data that is extracted but
  not yet swept for flag statistics.
- **Evidence searched**: the 9 borg-family records (all flagsB=0, flagsA in {0,1}); generic
  table DAT_802c4760 extracted to research/decomp/data/damage-records-802d46e0.json but not
  flag-audited; per-move hit-bin records not swept.
- **Next search/trace**: script sweep over user-data hit-bin extractions counting flag bits
  per move/borg (pure local data work, no ROM re-derivation).
- **Expected output**: flag histogram + list of exempt/piercing moves -> feeds ATK-008/014.
- **Agent type**: code (script sweep) — good cheap-agent task.

rag_vectors.name: Q11. Knockback magnitude and reaction lengths (pre-existing, re-ranked here)
rag_vectors.text:
# [research/decomp/attack-mechanics-open-questions.md] Q11. Knockback magnitude and reaction lengths (pre-existing, re-ranked here)
## Q11. Knockback magnitude and reaction lengths (pre-existing, re-ranked here)

- **Why it matters**: largest feel gap in already-ported systems; blocks MELEE/SHOT/SPECIAL
  KNOCKBACK/HITSTUN TUNED markers (constants.ts).
- **Evidence searched**: exhaustively in behavior-notes (p)/(s)/(u) — direction derived,
  magnitude consumer never found.
- **Next search/trace**: Dolphin T9 (fit launch speed vs record hitStrength 1/4/16).
- **Expected output**: DERIVED magnitude rule.
- **Agent type**: **Dolphin** (T9), then code to port the fit.
